license_note: CC BY-NC 4.0 — suitable for research/university demo; check terms for commercial use.
training_data: Ecologist-labelled quoll crops; five focal IDs with ≥40 crops each after quality filters (min short side, blur variance). Long-tail individuals excluded from this MVP gallery.
method: Per-ID prototype = L2-normalized mean of training embeddings; query classified by cosine similarity to prototypes. Optional UNKNOWN if top1 similarity or top1−top2 gap is below tuned thresholds.
metrics_closed_set_rank1: ≈61–69% (held-out test crops, varies slightly with split/filters)
metrics_with_unknown_gate: When the system abstains on uncertain matches (UNKNOWN), accuracy on accepted predictions is typically ≈84–91% depending on thresholds; trade-off: higher precision → more UNKNOWNs.
why_not_higher: Small number of identities but high within-class variation (IR vs daylight, partial body, blur).
Frozen generic animal re-ID features are not fine-tuned on this quoll population.
Single mean prototype per ID loses multi-modal appearance (pose, lighting).
Some pairs are visually confusable (see confusion matrix in evaluation logs).
scripts: scripts/train_megadescriptor_5quolls.py — smaller ResNet baseline on 5 folders
